title: Kerala reports fourth snakebite death in a week; 65-year-old woman dies in Alappuzha
content: In the fourth reported snakebite death in Kerala, a 65-year-old woman died in Alappuzha on late Friday. The deceased Indira is a native of Nedumudi. The incident occurred outside the bathroom adjacent to her house in the evening. She was immediately rushed to Vandanam Medical College Hospital, but died while undergoing treatment. According to officials from the medical college, Indira arrived at the casualty around 6.30 pm along with her relatives. She said it was a cobra that bit her. As soon as she reached, she collapsed. We administered anti-venom and intubated her. As her condition worsened, she was shifted to the Medicine ICU, but she passed away while under treatment. Her body was shifted for postmortem at 10 pm, he said. After the postmortem examination, the body will be handed over to her relatives. Indira is survived by her husband, Raghu. Meanwhile, another case of snakebite was reported from Nenmara in Palakkad on Saturday, where the individual is in stable condition. The incident comes two days after another Alappuzha native died following a suspected snakebite on Thursday. The deceased, Seleena, was a native of Cheravally in Kayamkulam. The incident occurred around 7.15 pm, about 3 km from her house, as she was about to get into her car after attending a relative's wedding reception. Kayamkulam Taluk Hospital RMO said preliminary check-ups did not indicate a snakebite, due to which anti-venom was not administered.
